history.directive: Focus priorities: (1) continuously test and improve the site (functionality, features, look & feel), (2) continuously find new worker leads, (3) prioritize top-notch SEO across the site.
Stop the MVP admin dashboard work. Founder does not want it built.
Add a roadmap item for a leads page where leads can be viewed by category (and related filters).
Bump Leads Directory Page priority to top and implement immediately.

Professional pages are shipping broken image URLs (Unsplash 404s), e.g. /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos and /professionals/maria-georgiou-ydravlikos. Replace dead image sources immediately and add image URL validation in build so no profile card can publish with a broken hero/avatar.

Professional profile UX is unacceptable: pages show failure-state copy to users (e.g. 'Αξιοπιστία: προσωρινά χαμηλή λόγω αποτυχίας φόρτωσης', 'Σφάλμα φόρτωσης κριτικών'). Hide internal failure diagnostics from public UI, implement graceful fallback for reviews, and show a clean empty-state instead of panic messaging.

Homepage and key landing pages are missing social preview essentials (no og:image, no twitter:card). Add complete Open Graph + Twitter metadata with a real branded image so links don’t look broken/cheap when shared.

The 404 experience is broken for SEO and trust: typo URLs like /profissionals return a page without title/description. Ship a proper custom 404 with meaningful <title>, meta description, clear navigation back to categories/home, and index control.

City service pages are near-clones (e.g. /episkevi-klimatistikou-athina vs /episkevi-klimatistikou-thessaloniki are ~96% identical text). Rewrite with real local differentiation (neighborhood coverage, local pricing realities, local proof/reviews) or you’re creating doorway-style thin pages that look programmatic and low-trust.

Σελίδα /professionals: η 3η κάρτα επαγγελματία (Γιάννης Κώστα) εμφανίζεται χωρίς φωτογραφία και αφήνει άδειο λευκό block στην κορυφή. Διόρθωσε το source της εικόνας και βάλε αξιόπιστο fallback image + fixed aspect ratio ώστε να μη διαλύεται οπτικά η κάρτα.

Σελίδα /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos: η κεντρική φωτογραφία προφίλ είναι broken (φαίνεται alt text αντί για εικόνα). Αυτό σκοτώνει εμπιστοσύνη. Διόρθωσε asset path/CDN και πρόσθεσε server-side validation ώστε να μην δημοσιεύεται προφίλ με νεκρή εικόνα.

Σελίδα /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos: στο section 'Αξιολογήσεις' εμφανίζεται κόκκινο μήνυμα σφάλματος validation πριν ο χρήστης κάνει οποιαδήποτε ενέργεια. Μην δείχνεις errors από προεπιλογή — ενεργοποίησε validation μόνο μετά από submit/interaction.

Homepage (mobile): το badge 'PREMIUM MARKETPLACE FOR HOME SERVICES' είναι στα αγγλικά ενώ όλη η σελίδα είναι στα ελληνικά. Αυτό δείχνει πρόχειρο template και όχι ελληνικό brand. Κάνε πλήρη γλωσσική ενοποίηση στα ελληνικά ή βάλε language toggle με συνεπή copy strategy.

Homepage: το page length είναι υπερβολικό (τεράστιο endless list υπηρεσιών) και θυμίζει dump περιεχομένου αντί για marketplace landing. Σπάσε την πληροφορία με ισχυρά φίλτρα, pagination/accordion ανά κατηγορία και lazy loading για να μειωθεί το αρχικό βάρος και το scroll fatigue.
The site still feels templated. Research and execute a 10x upgrade to make it world-class with a unique brand identity. This is now the #1 priority.

Homepage canonical is wrong/inconsistent: / returns canonical http://100.95.148.70:4322/ while category pages canonicalize to https://greekhandy.gr/... . Pick one public host (production domain) and enforce it everywhere, plus add 301 redirects from IP host to canonical domain to avoid duplicate-indexing.

HTTP response headers are missing charset (content-type is just text/html). Greek text can degrade into mojibake in some clients/crawlers. Serve text/html; charset=utf-8 globally and verify decoding consistency on all templates.

High-intent public routes are hard-404ing right now: /blog and /epikoinonia. Either ship real pages immediately or remove all references/sitemap entries and 301 them to relevant live destinations. Dead-end pages kill trust.

Slug strategy is inconsistent and creates avoidable 404s: /katharismos fails while /katharismoi exists. Implement redirect aliases for singular/plural Greek category variants (and common transliteration variants) instead of punishing users for natural URL guesses.

Homepage ships an extreme HTML payload (~2.8MB, ~447 internal links in initial markup) that looks programmatic and bloated. Split long service/city lists behind progressive disclosure (accordion + pagination/load-more) and trim above-the-fold DOM so first load feels like a real product, not an SEO dump.
Homepage is endless scrolling and feels like an SEO dump. Redesign homepage information architecture to reduce scroll fatigue and improve UX while preserving SEO quality.

Homepage hero contrast is unacceptable (especially on mobile): headline/body text blends into the pale background and is barely readable. Rework hero typography/colors to pass WCAG contrast and remove the washed-out gradient aesthetic that looks broken.

A floating accessibility toolbar is rendering on top of content (seen across category/profile/blog pages), covering CTAs and text. Dock it unobtrusively or collapse it to a small trigger so it never blocks core UI.

/blog is still a thin placeholder ('coming soon' style) with no real articles, which looks unfinished and low-trust. Publish actual Greek guides immediately (with author/date/reading time) or remove blog from primary nav until content exists.

Category pages like /ydravlikoi are bloated with an endless grid of low-context service cards. Add meaningful grouping + filters (urgency, city, budget) and reduce initial card dump so users can choose services without fatigue.

Public forms are functionally broken: POST to /api/professionals/register, /api/reviews/submit, /api/bookings/submit, and /api/messages/submit returns HTTP 500 with a TypeError page. Replace crash behavior with proper server-side validation + user-safe error responses (4xx with inline field errors), and prove each form can submit without exposing stack traces.

You are shipping a development build to users. HTML includes thousands of `data-astro-source-file`/`data-astro-source-loc` attributes with absolute server paths (e.g. /home/weakwire/projects/greekhandy/src/...). Strip all debug/source-location attributes from production output immediately; this is unprofessional leakage and massive DOM bloat.

Error responses expose Vite dev artifacts (`/@vite/client` and raw `<title>TypeError</title>`). Configure production error handling so users get clean branded error pages, no dev scripts, no framework internals, no stack-style crash output.

Core SEO trust signal is missing: key pages (/, /professionals, /ydravlikoi, /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos) have no JSON-LD structured data. Implement schema.org markup now (Organization + WebSite on homepage, Service on category pages, LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService + aggregateRating on profile pages) so search snippets don’t look empty and generic.

Homepage is still shipping as a bloated dev dump: ~3.97MB HTML, ~1,924 internal links in initial markup, and ~10,381 `data-astro-source-*` debug attributes leaked to users. Strip all source-location attrs in production and redesign homepage IA so real users are not forced through absurd SEO-scroll sludge.

Critical SEO regression: homepage `/` and professional profile `/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos` render without `<title>` and without meta description. Add unique, human-readable title + description per template immediately or search snippets will look broken/empty.

Greek copy is rendering as mojibake (garbled characters) on live pages, especially mobile homepage and profile views. Stop shipping unreadable text: enforce UTF-8 end-to-end (headers + templates + assets) and add a deployment check that fails build when Greek strings are mis-encoded.

Professional profile contact flow is still broken: forms post to `/api/bookings/submit` and `/api/messages/submit`, but both endpoints return 404. Wire these routes to real handlers (or change form actions) and confirm successful submission UX instead of dead POSTs.

/professionals still shows a broken image card for Γιάννης Κώστα (blank hero with alt text), and his profile hero image also fails. This is a direct trust killer. Fix asset pipeline/CDN references and block publish when profile media cannot be fetched.

Mobile homepage hero is still unreadable: light cyan/white text on a pale background fails contrast and looks visually broken. Redesign the hero color system (background, heading, subtext, CTAs) to pass WCAG AA on real devices, not just desktop mocks.

/professionals and /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos are still shipping broken profile media (blank image block with alt text). This kills trust instantly. Fix media URLs, add hard fallback images, and block publishing any professional profile that fails image fetch validation.

A floating accessibility widget is still sitting on top of core content across listing/category/profile pages. Reposition it to a non-blocking docked trigger (or auto-hide) so it never covers copy, buttons, or forms.

Category pages (/ydravlikoi, /ilektrologoi) still dump a huge repetitive card wall with weak differentiation and no practical filtering. Add real decision controls (city, urgency, budget, availability) and reduce initial card load so users can find a service without endless scrolling.

Professional profile UX is confused and bloated: users are hit with multiple competing forms in one page (quick contact, booking request, direct message), which creates friction and distrust. Consolidate into one primary conversion flow with a clear CTA hierarchy and move secondary contact options behind toggles.

Homepage hero is unreadable on both desktop and mobile (white/cyan text on pale gray + glow blur). Fix contrast to WCAG-compliant levels, remove the haze effect, and tighten spacing so core value proposition is legible within 2 seconds.

Top navigation ships broken IA: 'Οδηγοί' and 'Επαγγελματίες' currently return 404 from the live menu. Point menu items to real routes (or publish those pages) and add an automated test that fails deploys on nav links returning non-200.

Service taxonomy is inconsistent and exposes dead pages: category route '/thermansi' returns 404 while Heating appears as a promoted service path. Align slugs across homepage, footer, and category data so every promoted category resolves to a valid landing page.

Category listing cards look synthetic and untrustworthy (repetitive placeholder-like provider names, duplicated patterns, no real credibility signals). Replace fake-looking entries with validated marketplace data: consistent naming, service area clarity, review count provenance, and realistic profile snippets.

Lead forms on category pages lack basic trust/compliance UX: no explicit privacy consent checkbox, no clear personal-data notice near submit, and no expectation-setting after submission. Add GDPR-compliant consent text + policy link, and show clear success/failure states with response time expectations.

Mobile navigation is broken and looks unfinished: on the 375px homepage viewport the header only shows two links ('Οδηγοί', 'Επαγγελματίες') while core routes like 'Αρχική', 'Υπηρεσίες', and 'Επικοινωνία' disappear with no menu toggle. Implement a proper mobile nav (hamburger/drawer) so all primary routes are always reachable.

/professionals is still shipping a broken profile image card for Γιάννης Κώστα (blank white image block + alt text). This is a direct trust failure. Fix the media source and enforce pre-publish image validation with a real fallback thumbnail.

Floating accessibility toolbar still overlays core content (seen on professionals, blog, and contact pages), including over footer links/text. Reposition it to a non-blocking fixed edge trigger and enforce collision-safe spacing so it never covers actionable UI.

Category listing content still reads like synthetic filler: repeated low-credibility card naming patterns and near-identical snippets make the marketplace feel fake. Replace templated entries with credible provider data (distinct naming, proof signals, realistic service blurbs) before indexing these pages aggressively.

Service taxonomy/copy is still inconsistent: route '/thermansi' renders with title 'Τεχνικοί Κλιματισμού', mixing heating intent with AC branding. Align slug, H1, metadata, and body copy to one clear service intent or users and search engines will treat it as sloppy, mismatched landing-page spam.

Top-nav route '/messages' is publicly exposed but opens in a broken state for normal users (requires hidden query param `viewerEmail` and shows internal-style warnings). Remove this route from primary nav until it has a real user entry flow, or implement a proper authenticated inbox landing without technical parameter errors.

Homepage hero is still visually broken: ultra-light headline/body text on pale gray background is unreadable on both desktop and mobile. Rebuild hero contrast/typography hierarchy to pass WCAG AA and make the value proposition legible within 2 seconds.

Contact/navigation IA is inconsistent and sloppy: header uses anchor-based 'Επικοινωνία' (`/#contact`) while a standalone `/epikoinonia` page also exists. Pick one canonical contact destination, align all nav/footer links to it, and 301 the duplicate path to avoid fragmented UX and SEO signals.

Professional listing still publishes broken media for Γιάννης Κώστα (blank image frame with alt text in `/professionals`). This is a direct trust killer. Enforce hard image validation pre-publish and always render a branded fallback thumbnail when fetch fails.

Professional profile page (`/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos`) is conversion-chaos: users are hit with stacked forms (reviews + booking + direct message) in one long page with no clear primary CTA. Collapse to one dominant action path and demote secondary forms behind tabs/accordions to reduce friction.

Mobile homepage header is sloppy and confusing: it shows both a standalone 'ΜΕΝΟΥ' label and a separate '☰ Μενού' button at the same time. Remove the duplicate label and ship one clear mobile navigation trigger with consistent spacing/alignment.

Professional profile page `/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos` still leaks internal validation/debug copy directly into public UI (e.g. inline technical warnings in booking/reviews sections). Stop exposing system-style error text to users; show clean Greek microcopy and only after user interaction/submit.

Service page `/ydravlikoi` has an empty 'Σχετικές Υπηρεσίες' section ('Δεν υπάρχουν σχετικές υπηρεσίες για την ώρα'), which makes the page feel unfinished. Either populate real related services dynamically or remove the section entirely until it has content.

Floating accessibility toolbar is still physically covering page content/controls across key pages (`/blog`, `/allagi-kleidarias`, `/ydravlikoi`, profile pages). Dock it to a non-overlapping edge trigger and enforce collision-safe spacing so it never sits on top of CTAs/text.

Homepage hero is still barely readable (very light text on pale background, especially on mobile), so first impression looks broken. Rebuild hero contrast and typography hierarchy to pass WCAG AA and make core value proposition legible instantly.

Stop shipping Astro debug source attributes to production/dev preview. Every page is leaking `data-astro-source-file` paths (e.g. `/home/weakwire/projects/greekhandy/...`) and bloating HTML massively. Disable source annotations in build output and strip all `data-astro-source-*` attributes before deploy.

Homepage and major category pages are absurdly heavy HTML payloads (homepage ~1.08MB, /ydravlikoi ~1.78MB before assets). This is unacceptable for mobile users on Greek networks. Cut server-rendered markup size aggressively: paginate/limit long lists, lazy-render below-the-fold blocks, and remove duplicated hidden content.

Fix broken local SEO infrastructure: `/robots.txt` and `/sitemap.xml` both return 404. Add valid robots and sitemap endpoints immediately, include all indexable pages, and verify they return 200 with correct content-type.

Your location template has a grammar duplication bug in metadata and headings (e.g. `στην Πάτρα στην Πάτρα`, `στην Αθήνα στην Αθήνα` on pages like `/allagi-diakopton-prizon-patra` and `/episkevi-diarrois-stegi-amesa-athina`). Refactor title/meta generation to prevent repeated city injection and re-generate affected pages.

Port 4322 is down while QA instructions and agent workflow point to it as primary. Align runtime with documented port strategy: either make 4322 reliably serve the site or update all operational docs/automation to 4321. Right now this creates false downtime and wasted QA cycles.

Mobile homepage still looks broken and low-trust: hero copy is nearly invisible (very light text on pale background), so the core value proposition is unreadable on first view. Rebuild mobile hero contrast/typography (background, heading, body, CTA states) to pass WCAG AA and make the message legible instantly.

Mobile header is still shipping duplicated navigation affordances ('ΜΕΝΟΥ' label + separate '☰ Μενού' button). Remove the redundant label and keep one clear, accessible trigger; right now it looks like unfinished UI and wastes above-the-fold space.

Professional profile page (`/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos`) still shows inline validation error copy before meaningful user interaction (booking section warning text appears in default view). Do not dump error/validation messaging on initial load; trigger validation only after submit/field interaction and show clean user-facing microcopy.

Port strategy is still broken operationally: primary QA endpoint `:4322` is down while site serves on `:4321`. Either make `:4322` reliably live or officially switch all QA/docs/automation to `:4321`; this mismatch keeps generating false outage noise and wasted QA cycles.

Professional listing cards still feel synthetic and low-credibility (initials-only avatar tiles across all featured pros instead of real photos/proof). Upgrade listing trust signals: require real profile photos or branded verified placeholders with clear provenance, plus stronger identity cues (review proof, completed jobs, verification context).

Homepage hero is still visually broken on desktop and mobile: primary headline/body text is nearly invisible against the pale background, so users cannot read the core value proposition. Rebuild the hero color/typography system to pass WCAG AA contrast and make the message readable in under 2 seconds.

The /professionals grid still looks fake and templated: every card shows 'VERIFIED PLACEHOLDER' with initials-only avatars instead of credible identity. Replace synthetic placeholder presentation with real profile media or clearly branded fallback assets, and surface stronger trust proof (real review evidence, verification context, completed jobs).

Professional profile page /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos has a broken UI block in 'ΒΑΣΙΚΕΣ ΥΠΗΡΕΣΙΕΣ ΠΡΟΦΙΛ' (oversized blue pill/overlay with unreadable content). Fix layout/styles so service info is actually legible and remove any overflow artifact that makes the section look corrupted.

Same profile page ships unfinished portfolio visuals (empty/broken media blocks and generic placeholder avatar panel), which kills credibility for a paid marketplace. Require real portfolio assets per pro or hide the section until valid media exists; do not publish half-empty showcase components.

Public /messages page still exposes internal thread jargon and mixed-language UX ('MESSAGING ENTRY', 'thread id', 'viewer email δεν δόθηκε') to anonymous users. Replace this with a clean Greek user-facing flow (or gate behind authentication) and remove technical diagnostics from public copy.

Σελίδα /professionals: οι κάρτες γράφουν ξεκάθαρα 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER'. Αυτό είναι αυτογκόλ αξιοπιστίας και δείχνει demo, όχι marketplace. Αφαίρεσε κάθε placeholder label από public UI και εμφάνισε μόνο πραγματική κατάσταση επαλήθευσης με καθαρό ελληνικό copy.

Σελίδα /professionals: εμφανίζεις 'Δείκτης εμπιστοσύνης 0/100' και 'Εκπαίδευση κριτικών: 0%' σε όλους τους επαγγελματίες. Αυτά μοιάζουν με εσωτερικά QA metrics και καταστρέφουν την εμπιστοσύνη του χρήστη. Κρύψε τα μέχρι να υπάρχουν πραγματικά δεδομένα και κράτα μόνο χρήσιμα δημόσια trust signals.

Σελίδα /professionals/register: η φόρμα εγγραφής επαγγελματία δεν έχει checkbox συναίνεσης ούτε σύνδεσμο σε πολιτική απορρήτου πριν το submit. Πρόσθεσε υποχρεωτή GDPR συναίνεση και σαφή ενημέρωση για χρήση/αποθήκευση στοιχείων πριν το κουμπί 'Αποστολή Αίτησης'.

Σελίδα /messages: στην κατάσταση χωρίς ενεργή συνομιλία δείχνεις παραπλανητικό primary CTA 'Συνέχεια' και δευτερεύον 'Δοκιμή ξανά' που δεν εξηγούν τι θα γίνει. Κράτα ένα ξεκάθαρο action ('Μετάβαση σε Επαγγελματίες') και γράψε ακριβές αποτέλεσμα ανά κουμπί για να μη νιώθει ο χρήστης ότι πατάει τυφλά.

Mobile homepage: το hero badge παραμένει στα αγγλικά ('GREEKHANDY SIGNATURE NETWORK') ενώ το υπόλοιπο UI είναι ελληνικό. Αυτή η γλωσσική ασυνέπεια δείχνει πρόχειρο template. Κάνε πλήρη ελληνική προσαρμογή στο hero (badge, headings, supporting copy) ή πρόσθεσε πραγματικό language switch με συνεπές localization.

Homepage is leaking internal QA/instructional copy into public UI (e.g. blocks like 'Πρώτη οθόνη με προτεραιότητα στην αναγνωσιμότητα...' under the hero). Remove all editorial/dev-note text from production templates immediately; users should never see implementation commentary.

Homepage hero remains unreadable on real devices: ultra-light heading/body text over pale gray with glow blur makes the core value proposition barely visible. Rebuild hero contrast/typography to WCAG AA and kill the haze effect so the message is legible in under 2 seconds.

/professionals still screams fake marketplace: profile cards display explicit labels like 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER' and initials-only avatar tiles. Strip placeholder language from public UI and replace with real media or clearly branded fallback components that don’t look like unfinished demo data.

/professionals cards are exposing internal trust-framework filler copy ('ΠΛΑΙΣΙΟ ΑΞΙΟΠΙΣΤΙΑΣ' with generic rules text) instead of user-useful proof. Replace this with real credibility signals (verified identity status, real review evidence, completed jobs) or hide the block until genuine data exists.

/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos ships an empty 'Portfolio' container (large blank media area), which looks broken and unprofessional. If no assets exist, hide the section and show a concise empty-state; do not render dead visual blocks on live profile pages.

Homepage hero still fails basic readability: headline/body are ultra-light on a pale background (desktop + mobile), so the core value proposition is barely legible. Redesign hero contrast and typography hierarchy to pass WCAG AA and remove the glow haze that makes text look blurred.

/professionals still looks like staging data, not a trustworthy marketplace: cards expose labels like 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER' and trust metrics sitting at 0/100 across profiles. Remove all placeholder/filler language from public cards and hide credibility widgets until they are backed by real data.

Professional profile /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos still ships a broken credibility layout: large empty portfolio area and stacked conversion forms create visual clutter and low trust. Hide empty media sections and simplify to one primary contact flow instead of dumping multiple competing forms in sequence.

Form handling is misleading and opaque: invalid POSTs to /api/professionals/register return HTTP 200 and redirect users to a thank-you page with `status=invalid`, while other invalid form submits bounce to /professionals with query flags. Implement explicit validation UX with field-level errors and truthful success/failure states instead of faux-success redirects.

Primary QA endpoint is still broken: port 4322 refuses connections while the site only loads on 4321. Stop the operational chaos—either make 4322 reliably serve the app or update every QA instruction/automation to 4321 immediately.

You are publishing duplicate guide hub pages: /blog and /odigoi render essentially the same content/title, which creates SEO cannibalization and sloppy IA. Choose one canonical URL, 301 the duplicate, and align nav/sitemap/internal links accordingly.

Homepage still leaks internal editorial/instructional text into public UI (e.g. implementation-style copy blocks under hero). Remove all dev-note/process copy from production templates—users should only see customer-facing content.

/professionals still looks like fake staging data: cards expose placeholder-style identity/trust elements (e.g. initials tiles + generic 'framework' trust copy + zeroed metrics). Remove synthetic trust widgets from public cards until they are backed by real data and proof.

Professional profile page (/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos) still ships an empty Portfolio container and a cluttered multi-form conversion stack. Hide empty media sections and reduce to one primary contact path instead of dumping competing forms in sequence.

Mobile homepage hero still fails basic readability: the main headline/body text is too low-contrast against the pale background and looks washed out on first screen. Rework mobile hero colors + typography to pass WCAG AA and make the value proposition instantly legible.

/professionals still reads like fake staging data: cards expose 'ΕΠΑΛΗΘΕΥΜΕΝΟ PLACEHOLDER' plus zeroed trust metrics (0/100), which destroys marketplace credibility. Remove placeholder labels/metrics from public UI until backed by real verified data.

Professional profile page (/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos) still renders a large empty Portfolio block. Hide the portfolio section when no assets exist and show a compact empty-state instead of a dead gray container.

You are serving duplicate guide hubs as separate indexable URLs: /blog and /odigoi both return 200 with effectively identical content while canonical points to /blog. 301 redirect /odigoi to /blog (or differentiate content) to stop duplicate-content cannibalization.

Response headers are inconsistent and sloppy: homepage `/` is still served as `text/html` without `charset=utf-8`, while other pages include UTF-8. Enforce `text/html; charset=utf-8` on every HTML route to prevent Greek encoding regressions in crawlers/clients.

Route alias quality is sloppy: `/epaggelmaties` returns HTTP 200 with effectively the same content as `/professionals` (same title/H1/content, only canonical differs). Stop serving duplicate hubs as indexable 200 pages—301 redirect `/epaggelmaties` to `/professionals` and keep one clean URL.

Form failure UX is still broken and misleading: invalid POSTs to `/api/reviews/submit`, `/api/bookings/submit`, and `/api/messages/submit` 303-redirect users to generic `/professionals?...=invalid` instead of returning to the originating profile section with inline field errors. Keep users in-context, preserve inputs, and show precise error states next to the actual form they submitted.

Public `/messages` flow still leaks internal parameter semantics (`viewerEmail`) into user-facing behavior/state handling. Replace query-param-driven diagnostics with a real entry flow (auth/session-backed inbox or clear handoff), and remove any technical parameter language from public UX.

Response headers are still inconsistent across core routes: homepage `/` is served as `text/html` without `charset=utf-8`, while key inner pages send UTF-8 explicitly. Standardize all HTML responses to `text/html; charset=utf-8` to prevent Greek text decoding regressions and crawler misinterpretation.
